commit.message:
feat: add 5 mandatory verification stages (6-10 / 10-14)

Stage 6/10: Product Review vs PRD — 10 critics against acceptance
criteria, must pass with score >0, 0 Critical, 0 Warnings

Stage 7/11: E2E Local — run E2E suite against local dev server,
loop until all green (max 3 iterations)

Stage 8/12: Deploy to Staging — run staging.deploy_command from
pipeline.config.yaml, verify staging URL is reachable

Stage 9/13: Tests vs Staging — full test suite against staging URL

Stage 10/14: E2E vs Staging — same E2E suite as local, pointed at
staging URL, with local vs staging comparison

Added staging config hook (staging.deploy_command, staging.url) to
pipeline-config-template.yaml. Updated state file schemas in all
three files (fullpipeline, tdd-fullpipeline, clear_and_go).

commit.message:
feat: comprehensive codebase audit — security, performance, accessibility, and code quality

Security: cascade workspace deletion, assignee membership validation, input bounds
enforcement, CSS injection prevention (isSafeCoverValue), comment membership re-check.
Performance: denormalized userName/userImageUrl on activities and comments, logActivity()
helper, .take() query limits, composite indexes (by_board_archived, by_user).
Accessibility: aria-labels on icon buttons, keyboard handlers on role="button" divs,
focus:opacity-100 on hover-only elements, lazy loading on cover images.
Code quality: centralized color palettes in lib/colors.ts, safe JSON.parse in editor,
typed assigneeIds, removed manual useCallback for React Compiler compatibility,
error boundaries and loading skeletons for document routes, removed unused tw-animate-css.

commit.message:
feat: restructure income statement to USAR restaurant accounting standard (#420)

* fix: reorder migration timestamp to match deploy sequence

PR #408 (copy_week_shifts at 20260303200000) was deployed before
PR #409 (restaurant_financial_settings at 20260303130000), causing
`supabase db push` to fail with an out-of-order migration error.

Renamed 20260303130000 → 20260303200001 so it sorts after the
already-applied migration.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: address CodeRabbit findings — throw errors, fix date format, escape CSV

- useUncategorizedTotals: throw on query errors instead of swallowing
  so React Query handles retries and error states
- useUncategorizedTotals: use date-fns format() instead of
  toISOString().split('T')[0] to avoid UTC off-by-one
- IncomeStatement CSV export: escape values containing commas/quotes
- Update unit test to verify error-throwing behavior

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: address CodeRabbit iteration 2 — error state, null guards, EBITDA, timeout

- Expose error/isError from useUncategorizedTotals hook
- Add null guard to export handlers, remove non-null assertions
- Include amortization in EBITDA calculation (D&A, not just D)
- Add timeout to negative EBITDA visibility assertion in E2E

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: replace PostgREST aggregate query with client-side aggregation

PostgREST does not support aggregate functions (sum/count) in select.
Fetch raw amounts and aggregate client-side instead.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: add pagination to uncategorized totals query for busy restaurants

PostgREST has a 1000-row default limit. Paginate with .range()
to ensure all transactions are counted.

recording discovery and playback. DB migrations add last_recorded_at
and recording_path columns to demo_scenarios and feedback_sessions.

commit.message:
fix: debug y corregir clasificación stock en tablero facturación

Bug: isItemReady() usaba SOLO stock real, ignorando deliveryTime.
Si stockQuantity era 0 (no null), el item siempre era "no listo",
incluso si el vendedor lo había marcado como "Inmediato".

Fix: un item está listo si CUALQUIERA de estas condiciones se cumple:
1. deliveryTime es "Inmediato" (el vendedor confirma disponibilidad)
2. stockQuantity >= remainingQuantity (stock real suficiente)

Esto combina ambas señales: el vendedor puede saber que hay stock
aunque no esté reflejado en el sistema (ej: stock en tránsito).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix PWA sign-in: use signInWithRedirect in standalone mode

signInWithPopup opens a separate browser tab in iOS PWA which never
returns to the app. Use signInWithRedirect instead when running as a
PWA, and handle the result via getRedirectResult on page load.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix burger menu z-index on all pages + tron menu z-index on dashboard

The mobile burger menu drawer (gnav-links) was invisible when opened because
it used position:fixed without a z-index, so page content covered it.
Added z-index:9001 to all 13 pages (12 inline + style.css).

Dashboard tron-menu-btn was hidden behind gnav (z-index 5001 < 9000).
Raised tron-menu-btn to 9002, backdrop to 9003, drawer to 9004.

Also adds screenshot_audit.js tool for automated UI/UX testing.

Replace Axum with raw io_uring HTTP server in mahalo-endpoint

Swap out the Axum/Hyper HTTP stack for a custom io_uring event loop with
thread-per-core architecture, SO_REUSEPORT, and CPU pinning to eliminate
tokio work-stealing overhead and target Actix-level throughput.

New modules:
- http_parse: httparse-based request parsing + response serialization
- uring: io_uring event loop, BufferPool (8KB×4096), ConnectionPool (10K),
  user-data encoding (state/slot/generation in u64)
- worker: thread-per-core spawner with SQPOLL fallback, per-worker
  current-thread tokio runtime for async handler execution

WebSocket support deferred — mahalo-channel retains its own axum dep.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
fix: embedded ICMP fabric redirect after NAT rewrite (split-RG)

In split-RG cluster, ICMP Time Exceeded from transit routers arrives
on the WAN node but must reach the LAN host on the peer node.
handle_embedded_icmp_v4/v6() was calling try_fabric_redirect() BEFORE
NAT rewrite — the peer received packets with SNAT'd embedded headers
and couldn't match them to any session, silently dropping them.

Fix: when FIB fails (UNREACHABLE/BLACKHOLE) for the original client,
set META_FLAG_KERNEL_ROUTE instead of attempting immediate fabric
redirect. xdp_forward's KERNEL_ROUTE path re-FIBs using post-NAT
packet headers (outer dst rewritten to original client IP), detects
UNREACHABLE, and fabric-redirects with correct headers.

Also change meta_flags assignment from = to |= to preserve
KERNEL_ROUTE flag when setting META_FLAG_EMBEDDED_ICMP.

Tested: split-RG MTR shows all intermediate hops (was 100% loss),
make test-failover 14/14 pass at 7.86 Gbps.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
fix: handle markdown links in YAML frontmatter values (#255)

* fix: handle markdown links in YAML frontmatter values (#254)

The YAML fallback parser only quoted `name` and `description` fields,
so markdown links like `author: [Name](url)` in other fields crashed
the entire repo processing. Broaden the fallback to quote any value
containing `](` (markdown link pattern) and add `yaml.YAMLError` to
`discover_skills` exception handler as defense-in-depth.

* docs: update parse_frontmatter_yaml docstring to reflect broadened fallback

commit.message:
fix: move completeOnboarding to tour end to prevent remount

completeOnboarding() calls revalidatePath('/dashboard') which causes
the Server Component to re-render and remount DashboardTour, resetting
the useTour() hook state and killing the tour mid-flight. Now
completeOnboarding is only called from DashboardTour after the tour
finishes (complete or skip), not from the welcome modal's Get Started.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

perf: aggressive GPU/CPU optimization for low-end AMD APU kiosk

Target hardware: AMD GX-217GA @ 1.38GHz, Radeon HD 8280E 512MB VRAM

Removed:
- BackgroundOrbs (3× motion.div with repeat:Infinity rAF loop + blur-[120px/150px/100px]
  CSS filter on 50-60vw divs) — biggest GPU killer, now replaced by
  StaticBackground: a single static div with CSS radial-gradient, zero JS/blur cost
- backdrop-blur-lg on grandmaster card
- All Framer Motion from static layout elements (header, grandmaster, section grid)
  — motion.div entrance animations replaced with plain div
- backdrop-blur-md from leaderboard message banner
- hover:bg-white/5 from Section rows (repaint on hover, no mouse on TV anyway)
- drop-shadow filter on score text in overlay (filter on large text is expensive)
- Spring animation on overlay → simple easeOut tween (cheaper, still smooth)
- Reduced box-shadow spread on overlay (150px → 100px)

Framer Motion now used ONLY for the overlay AnimatePresence (appears ~2×/game)

Result: continuous GPU load drops from 3 blur-animated large layers to near-zero
during idle display. Score countdown and CSS scroll animations unaffected.
